verdict: Bounded 93 candidate clears the internal control-theory bar because the claim tier is now tied to the parity gate rather than to flat equal-domain rhetoric.
Runtime budgets, lifecycle breadth, and deployment scope are now explicit enough for a bounded flagship submission.
The bounded monograph now separates formal calibration from conservative widening clearly enough to score above the internal readiness bar.
Bounded deployment language, explicit open-row blockers, and the new scope table move the package into low-90s submission territory without overstating field readiness.
The monograph is now internally ready as a bounded universal-safety submission even though equal-domain closure still remains a separate gate.
Equal-domain promotion still fails the internal bar because navigation and aerospace remain blocked at the parity gate.
The runtime layer is strong, but the all-domain closure target remains incomplete until the two blocked rows are defended on real data.
Equal-domain statistical closure is still unavailable while navigation lacks a defended real-data row and aerospace lacks a real flight benchmark.
The equal-peer deployment reading is still blocked by the same two domain closures and should remain blocked.
The book is structurally ready, but the equal-domain version is not yet defensible until the parity blockers are cleared.
